title: USS Nimitz / Tic-Tac
location: Pacific Ocean, ~100 nm SW of San Diego, CA
summary: Navy F/A-18F pilots from VFA-41 intercept a 40-foot white Tic-Tac-shaped object with no visible propulsion, wings, or exhaust. It demonstrated instantaneous acceleration from 28,000 ft to sea-level and back. Princeton's SPY-1 radar had been tracking these objects for two weeks prior.

title: Gimbal
summary: USS Theodore Roosevelt carrier group pilots film a rotating, oblong UAP moving against the wind with no visible propulsion. The object appears to rotate in a manner inconsistent with any known aircraft. Published by NYT in 2017.

title: GoFast
summary: A fast-moving UAP tracked over ocean surface by F/A-18 ATFLIR pod. Analysis suggests the object was traveling at extraordinary speed at very low altitude with no discernible wake or propulsion signature.

title: Ariel School, Ruwa Zimbabwe
summary: 62 schoolchildren reported a craft landing on school grounds during recess. Children independently described occupants and received what they interpreted as telepathic warnings about environmental destruction. Harvard psychiatrist Dr. John Mack conducted months-long interviews and found no evidence of fabrication.

title: Lubbock Lights
summary: Three Texas Tech professors simultaneously observe a V-formation of soft blue-green lights passing overhead at high speed. Multiple subsequent sightings photographed by student Carl Hart Jr. Project Blue Book investigated and classified the photos as authentic but offered no explanation.

title: Rendlesham Forest
summary: US Air Force personnel stationed at RAF Bentwaters encountered a triangular craft in Rendlesham Forest over two consecutive nights. Deputy Base Commander Lt. Col. Charles Halt recorded audio during the second encounter and filed an official memo. Radiation readings at the landing site were 10x background.
